original_query: select  c_customer_id as customer_id        , coalesce(c_last_name,'') || ', ' || coalesce(c_first_name,'') as customername  from customer      ,customer_address      ,customer_demographics      ,household_demographics      ,income_band      ,store_returns  where ca_city	        =  'Pleasant Hill'    and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and ib_lower_bound   >=  35577    and ib_upper_bound   <=  35577 + 50000    and ib_income_band_sk = hd_income_band_sk    and cd_demo_sk = c_current_cdemo_sk    and hd_demo_sk = c_current_hdemo_sk    and sr_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk  order by c_customer_id  limit 100;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    c.c_customer_id AS customer_id,
    COALESCE(c.c_last_name, '') || ', ' || COALESCE(c.c_first_name, '') AS customername
FROM 
    customer c
    JOIN customer_address ca ON c.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk
    JOIN customer_demographics cd ON cd.cd_demo_sk = c.c_current_cdemo_sk
    JOIN household_demographics hd ON hd.hd_demo_sk = c.c_current_hdemo_sk
    JOIN income_band ib ON ib.ib_income_band_sk = hd.hd_income_band_sk
    JOIN store_returns sr ON sr.sr_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
WHERE 
    ca.ca_city = 'Pleasant Hill'
    AND ib.ib_lower_bound >= 35577
    AND ib.ib_upper_bound <= 85577
ORDER BY 
    c.c_customer_id
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Convert old-style comma joins to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and to help the optimizer understand the join relationships better.
Replace the expression 'ib_upper_bound <= 35577 + 50000' with the pre-calculated value 'ib_upper_bound <= 85577'.
